lead_profile_text: EX-STL switching from Pass the Keys (20% fee). 3 bed house, Sneinton, Nottingham NG2. Started STL January 2026. After 4+ months with PTK: only 4 reviews, peak gross month ~£1,000 vs promised £1,500-2,000. Mortgage £480/month + ~£250/month bills. Stayful projecting £31,000 gross/yr, ~£16,319 net. LTL estimate £9,871/year. June booking of £1,500/week already confirmed validating nightly rate. Vanessa and partner are both teachers — income consistency is critical.
emotional_profile: Financially pressured teacher; values consistency and transparency over hype; burned by PTK underperformance and broken promises; motivated to make STL work but anxious about winter reliability and making another mistake; responds to honesty and concrete proof points
delay_trigger_phrases: considering her options before the 15 May deadline
it's the inconsistency that worries me
green_light_trigger_phrases: already taking the step into STL
the June booking shows it can work
I don't want to make another mistake with another company

top_objections.text: Will we actually hit the projected income with only 4 reviews right now?
top_objections.zac_response: June booking of £1,500/week already validates nightly rate; honest ramp-up acknowledgement given

top_objections.text: Is short-let really better than guaranteed long-let? We need consistency.
top_objections.zac_response: STL net £16,319 vs LTL £9,871 — £6,448/year advantage presented; Nottingham year-round demand fundamentals explained

top_objections.text: Why would Stayful be better than Pass the Keys who made similar promises?
top_objections.zac_response: 4-platform listing vs PTK Airbnb-only; Slack comms; directly employed team; dynamic pricing updated daily; 13% vs 20% fee

top_objections.text: What happens if projections aren't hit — especially January to April?
top_objections.zac_response: Nottingham demand: universities, QMC hospital, 1,000+ local events; honest seasonal context given

top_objections.text: What about the June £1,500/week booking — does switching management affect it?

top_questions.text: How does the 13% Stayful fee compare to Pass the Keys 20%?
top_questions.answer: 13%+VAT vs PTK 20% — significant saving clearly calculable on projected income figures

top_questions.text: What platforms will the property be listed on?
top_questions.answer: 4 platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com plus direct booking channels) vs PTK Airbnb-only

top_questions.text: What are the projected annual income figures?
top_questions.answer: £31,000 gross annually, ~£16,319 net to Vanessa after fees; vs LTL £9,871/year — £6,448/year STL advantage

what_moved_them: 4-platform listing vs Airbnb-only — tangible differentiator she had not expected
Honest acknowledgement that year 1 will be below full-potential while reviews build — trusted Stayful's candour vs PTK's promises
June booking of £1,500/week already in place — concrete proof point validating nightly rate and demand
Slack communication model vs poor PTK communication she has experienced
13% vs 20% fee — significant saving she could clearly calculate herself
what_fell_flat: No second meeting agreed — left decision entirely to lead with only deadline pressure
Winter income consistency concern not fully resolved — anxiety remains
Trust gap from PTK experience not fully bridged at meeting; credibility gap still open

conversion_gaps.gap: Trust gap — PTK made similar promises; Stayful must win on proof not promises
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Share verified case studies from comparable Nottingham EX-STL switches; concrete review velocity data; documented communication SLAs

conversion_gaps.gap: Winter income anxiety unresolved — Jan-Apr consistency concern
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Provide seasonal booking data for NG2 area; QMC hospital and Nottingham university year-round demand evidence

conversion_gaps.gap: No follow-up meeting booked — decision left to lead alone before 15 May deadline
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Always book a follow-up call before any offer expiry; never leave a switching decision to solo reflection without a scheduled touchpoint

offer_framing: 13% + VAT switching incentive vs standard 15% — explicit comparison to PTK's 20% fee; framed as reward for switching to a better operator, not desperation; valid until 15 May 2026
zac_income_intro: £31,000 projected gross annually, ~£16,319 net to Vanessa after Stayful fees, cleaning and platform costs — vs LTL £9,871/year, a £6,448/year advantage for short-letting
zac_reassurance_phrases: Honest acknowledgement that year 1 income will be below full-potential while reviews build
June £1,500/week booking is concrete proof that nightly rate and demand are already validated
